transcript.interviewer: What's something you've been thinking about lately?
transcript.interviewee: Just life, I guess. Work, where things are going. The usual stuff everyone thinks about.
transcript.interviewer: Anything specific underneath the usual stuff?
transcript.interviewee: Not really. I'm a pretty open book. What you see is mostly what there is — I don't think I'm hiding deep secret floors or anything.
transcript.interviewer: Has the way you see things changed over time?
transcript.interviewee: A bit, sure. Everyone changes a little. But I wouldn't call it a big before-and-after. No dramatic turning point, no old me versus new me. It's been pretty gradual and flat.
transcript.interviewer: Do you feel pulled between any two options right now?
transcript.interviewee: Not particularly. I'm not agonizing over a fork in the road. Things are kind of fine.
transcript.interviewer: Could you place yourself on a couple of axes — cautious versus bold, planner versus improviser?
transcript.interviewee: I mean, somewhere in the middle of both, probably. I don't really think about myself in those terms. I'm not strongly any of it.
transcript.interviewer: If you had to name the shape of where you are — a depth, a tension, a position, a journey?
transcript.interviewee: Honestly, none of them really fit, or maybe all of them a tiny bit. I'm just kind of an average person going through an average stretch. There's no strong shape to point at.
